title: quercus suber subf. clavata
intro: Quercus suber subf. clavata, also known as the Cork Oak, is a subspecies of the Quercus suber tree, characterized by its unique, more rounded cork bark. The bark of this plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. In addition to its medicinal properties, the Quercus suber subf. clavata is believed to have various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the cork oak bark can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
